channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Officials demand answers as crews work to restore power after another Puerto Rico blackout
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Crews worked early Thursday to restore power to Puerto Rico after a blackout across the entire island that affected the main international airport, several hospitals and hotels filled with Easter vacationers The outage that began past noon Wednesday left 1.4 million cust
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Divided loyalties? 62 Taiwanese military members found with Chinese residency permits
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — At least 62 active-duty Taiwanese military members have been found holding Chinese residency permits, the self-ruled island's Defense Ministry said, in the latest revelation of Chinese influence in the Taiwanese armed forces.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Power blackout hits all of Puerto Rico as residents prepare for Easter weekend
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — A power blackout hit all of Puerto Rico on Wednesday as the heavily Catholic U.S. territory prepared to celebrate the Easter weekend.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tennessee’s GOP leads the fight to deny public education to children without documents
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Damian Felipe Jimenez has many dreams about his future — he could be a restaurant owner, a scientist or maybe something else.
item.dc:creator: KIMBERLEE KRUESI and KRISTIN M. HALL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: San Francisco's new mayor is starting to unite the fractured city
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Democrats nationally may be in turmoil, but liberals in San Francisco are hopeful the new mayor's collaborative approach will help solve entrenched problems in a city recently known for its bitter infighting and chaotic streets.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR and OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge says labor unions' lawsuit over DOGE access to Labor Department systems can move forward
item.description: A federal judge says he won't dismiss a lawsuit from labor unions seeking to block Elon Musk's team from accessing systems at the Labor Department.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: NY prison guards beat an inmate to death then tried to cover it up, prosecutors say
item.description: UTICA, N.Y. (AP) — Ten New York prison guards were charged Wednesday in connection with the fatal beating of a 22-year-old inmate last month — including two charged with murder. It's the second time a group of correctional officers in the state was indicted for a death behind bars this year.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL and DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NY AG James referred to DOJ for alleged mortgage fraud
item.description: If proven, the claims could lead to prosecution for one of New York’s highest-ranking officials, who has long argued that “no one is above the law.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, NewsNation

item.title: Lost toddler found miles from home by dog that guided him to safety, sheriff says
item.description: When an Arizona toddler wandered away from home Monday, a team of deputies, search and rescue volunteers and rangers began their search. In the end, it was a protective pup who track down the 2-year-old.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Handyman named person of interest after missing woman's body found buried in concrete
item.description: A missing woman was found buried and partially encased in concrete more than a week after she went missing. Now, deputies are questioning her handyman for his possible role in her disappearance and death.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KRON4

item.title: Temu and Shein say they're raising prices due to tariffs
item.description: Both companies said they would be making “price adjustments” starting April 25, although neither provided details about the size of the increases.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Proposed rule change on endangered species triggers alarm for environmentalists
item.description: The Trump administration plans to rewrite part of the Endangered Species Act that prohibits harming the habitats of endangered and threatened species.
item.dc:creator: Tammy Webber, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How war, money and the quest for discovery entwined the US government and universities
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The showdown between the Trump administration and Harvard University is spotlighting bare-knuckled politics and big dollar figures. But in the battle of the moment, it’s easy to lose sight of a decades-long alliance between the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ADAM GELLER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ex-NYPD officer sentenced to 18 months after conviction for helping China stalk an expat
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former New York police sergeant was sentenced Wednesday to 18 months in prison in a U.S. case about China's pursuit of critics abroad, a sentence that came after two members of Congress urged the judge to spare him from time behind bars.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Texas DOGE calls for sanctions, audit of insurance company accused of spying
item.description: DOGE Lawmakers and Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton have accused Superior of hiring private investigators to get information on lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Dylan McKim, NewsNation

item.title: Family says ICE agents smashed car window in seizing Guatemalan man who's seeking asylum
item.description: NEW BEDFORD, Mass. (AP) — A Massachusetts family is demanding answers from U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement, complaining its agents smashed a car window with a hammer and detained a man who they claim had applied for asylum.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and RODRIQUE NGOWI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Walgreens class action drug settlement: Thursday deadline for $100M
item.description: The lawsuit alleges Walgreens sold generic drugs at a higher cost to customers with insurance than to Prescription Savings Club members, according to court docs.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: McDonald's teases the return of the Snack Wrap
item.description: McDonald's has finally teased a re-release date for a once-popular menu item. Kind of. In a relatively cryptic post to X on Tuesday, the fast-food chain announced the beloved Snack Wraps would return on the 14th.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, KTLA 5

item.title: Biden, Obama-appointed judges oversee bulk of Trump cases
item.description: The Gavel is The Hill's courts newsletter. Sign up by clicking here or adding your email in the box below.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Border Patrol morale is 'going through the roof' under Trump administration, top official says
item.description: U.S. Border Patrol Chief Mike Banks said morale at his agency is "going through the roof" under the Trump administration and that recruiting numbers are up.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Slave descendants fight to protect their threatened island community at Georgia's highest court
item.description: SAVANNAH, Ga. (AP) — Georgia's highest court waded Wednesday into a fight between Black landowners and local officials who have weakened long-standing protections for one of the South's last Gullah-Geechee communities founded by freed slaves.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Harvard's challenge to Trump administration could test limits of government power
item.description: On one side is Harvard, the nation’s oldest and wealthiest university, with a brand so powerful that its name is synonymous with prestige. On the other side is the Trump administration, determined to go further than any other White House to reshape American higher education.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: AP Was There: A truck bomb rips through a federal building in Oklahoma City in 1995
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — EDITORS NOTE: On April 19, 1995, a former U.S. Army soldier parked a rented Ryder truck loaded with a powerful bomb made of fertilizer and fuel oil outside a federal office building in Oklahoma City. The blast at the Alfred P.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: White House removes wire spot from press pool
item.description: The White House is making changes to which outlets are included in the press pool covering President Trump, and doing away with the spot normally reserved for wire services covering his daily activities.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KRON4

item.title: Starbucks introducing new dress code for baristas, a move protested by some
item.description: Baristas at your local Starbucks will soon be sporting a new, more consistent look after the company announced a new dress code this week.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KRON4

item.title: Wink Martindale, game show host and Elvis Presley interviewer, dies at 91
item.description: Wink Martindale, the genial host of such hit game shows as “Gambit” and “Tic-Tac-Dough” who also did one of the first recorded television interviews with a young Elvis Presley, has died. He was 91.
item.dc:creator: By BETH HARRIS Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: NC serial offender accused of mowing down teacher was in and out of 'revolving door' jail for 2 decades
item.description: A North Carolina repeat offender is back in jail on $5 million bond after he was charged with fatally striking a Durham teacher in a crash while he was not supposed to be driving.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Children on playgrounds targeted by drug runners with candy-colored 'trash cans,' DEA agent warns
item.description: Some drug dealers are moving toward selling drugs in small, brightly colored capsules called "trash cans." The containers have been found near schools and on playgrounds.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida man accused of killing girlfriend and her mother, dismembering one of the bodies
item.description: Kirk Hysell Jr., 41, is charged with two counts of first-degree murder after police say he stabbed his girlfriend and her mother to death in St. Petersburg, Florida.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Mexico vehicle crash claims lives of 2 US service members, 1 in serious condition
item.description: Two U.S. service members are dead and one is in serious condition after a vehicle crash while working in support of Joint Task Force Southern Border in New Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden alleges Trump has 'taken a hatchet' to Social Security in his first post-presidency speech
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Former President Joe Biden warned on Tuesday that President Donald Trump has “taken a hatchet” to Social Security, weighing in on a critical issue for millions of Americans as the 82-year-old Democrat briefly returned to the national stage.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Here's a behind-the-scenes look of the 'Wicked' Broadway tour as it flies into Utah
item.description: As "Wicked" returns to 'defy gravity' for Utah audiences, ABC4 got a peek backstage to see how the show all comes together.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Baggerly, ABC 4

item.title: No jurors picked on first day of Harvey Weinstein’s #MeToo retrial
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As jury selection began Tuesday in Harvey Weinstein’s New York City rape retrial, some prospective jurors made clear they couldn't be fair in judging the one-time Hollywood mogul-turned-#MeToo pariah.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Actor William Levy arrested after drunken 'disturbance' at Florida restaurant: report
item.description: Cuban-American actor William Levy was arrested in South Florida Monday after causing a "disturbance" inside a restaurant, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump not expected to attend White House Correspondents' dinner
item.description: President Donald Trump is not expected to attend next weekend's White House Correspondents' dinner, The Hill confirmed on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KTLA 5

item.title: Man allegedly held captive by stepmother in Connecticut releases first public statement
item.description: In his first public statement, the man allegedly held captive in his home for over 20 years by his stepmother says he's been getting stronger as he begins the process of reclaiming his life.
item.dc:creator: Bailey Wright, WGN 9

item.title: Trump administration freezes $2.2 billion in federal funds for Harvard University
item.description: The Trump administration froze $2.2 billion worth of federal money for Harvard University after the school said it would not cave to the White House’s orders to limit activism or political speech on campus.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Shirley, KTLA 5
